meta.title: Documentation — BuildTrack
topbar.label: DOCUMENTATION · BT-DOC
title: Module guides
intro: Short instructions, in the order they're used on site. This guide describes only what the system does today — no promises.
s0.title: Getting started
s0.sub: From zero to your first project loaded. Under 15 minutes.
s0.step.1.rest: Just two things: the company name and your admin account (your name, email and password). Nothing else.
s0.step.2.rest: Each person signs in with their email, with the role that fits — office, superintendent or field.
s0.step.3.rest: The name is enough. Client, contract amount and map location are optional — add them whenever you want.
s0.step.4.rest: The field crew clocks in and uploads photos from day one. iPhone coming soon.
s0.note: NOTE — AMOUNTS ARE SHOWN IN QUETZALES (Q).
s1.title: Projects
s1.sub: Every project is a file: contract, budget, changes and progress in one place.
s1.step.1.rest: Enter the amount and BuildTrack tracks the receivable balance on its own as you issue progress billings.
s1.step.2.rest: Foundation, structure, MEP, finishes — or your own. Physical progress is reported against those phases.
s1.step.3.rest: Every extra is numbered (CO-01, CO-02…) with its amount and approval. An approved change order can increase the contract amount — the balance recalculates on its own.
s1.step.4.bold: Reassign an invoice to another project
s1.step.4.rest: if it was logged on the wrong one. The expense moves with its full history.
s1.step.5.rest: With protection: a project with active data can't be deleted by accident.
s1.note: NOTE — PHYSICAL PROGRESS FEEDS FROM THE FIELD APP (BT-DOC-06); NO NEED TO ENTER IT TWICE.
s2.title: Financials
s2.sub: Five views: accounts payable, accounts receivable, labor, office expenses and budgets.
s2.a.label: 02.A — ACCOUNTS PAYABLE
s2.a.step.1.rest: with their due date and attach the invoice photo. You can filter everything by project.
s2.a.step.2.rest: Each account shows its full detail with the payment history. Amounts and dates can be edited.
s2.a.step.3.bold: Void a payment if you got it wrong
s2.a.step.3.rest: — the amount returns to the phase budget, no manual reconciling.
s2.a.step.4.bold: Convert an account into a numbered invoice
s2.a.step.4.rest: when the supplier issues it — the record stays linked.
s2.b.label: 02.B — ACCOUNTS RECEIVABLE
s2.b.step.1.bold: Issue progress billings to the client
s2.b.step.1.rest: for completed work and track them through to collection. The contract balance updates on its own.
s2.c.label: 02.C — LABOR & EXPENSES
s2.c.step.1.rest: from the field app's geofenced time clock — real hours, per person and per project.
s2.c.step.2.rest: separate from projects, so they don't muddy any project's cost.
s2.d.label: 02.D — BUDGETS
s2.d.step.1.bold: Compare contract vs. actual by phase
s2.d.step.1.rest: and see the cost distribution. If a phase is eating the margin, you see it before it hurts.
s3.title: Daily log & punch list
s3.sub: The daily log that protects your company, and the punch list that closes the project.
s3.step.1.rest: attendance is suggested from the time clock, and you add weather, the day's tasks and notes.
s3.step.2.rest: They stay in the day's entry, with a viewer to review them full-size, one by one.
s3.step.3.rest: numbered, with a photo, an assignee and a status — from open to resolved.
s3.step.4.bold: The client can also report punch items
s3.step.4.rest: from their portal — they enter the same list, numbered, so nothing gets lost in calls.
s4.title: RFIs
s4.sub: Formal questions to the client or owner, numbered and with the answer on record.
s4.step.1.bold: Create the RFI with a photo.
s4.step.1.rest: BuildTrack assigns a number (RFI-001, RFI-002…) and a response due date.
s4.step.2.bold: The client answers through a link
s4.step.2.rest: — nothing to install, no account. The conversation stays in writing, with date and author.
s4.step.3.bold: On closing, you pick the official answer
s4.step.3.rest: and record the cost and schedule impact, if any.
s4.step.4.bold: If the answer changes the cost,
s4.step.4.rest: the RFI links to a change order — the RFI and the CO stay connected.
s4.note: NOTE — «YOU TOLD ME OVER WHATSAPP» STOPS BEING AN ARGUMENT: EVERY RFI HAS A NUMBER, A DATE AND AN ANSWER.
s5.title: Client portal
s5.sub: The owner sees their project without calling you — no account, nothing to install.
s5.step.1.rest: for the project, with an optional PIN if you want an extra layer of access.
s5.step.2.bold: The client sees only THEIR project:
s5.step.2.rest: the daily log, punch list and RFIs. And they answer RFIs or report punch items right there.
s6.title: Field app
s6.sub: The field app: time clock, evidence and progress — with or without signal. Available for Android; iPhone coming soon.
s6.step.1.rest: a radius over the site on the map. Clock-ins only count inside that radius.
s6.step.2.bold: Each worker clocks in and out
s6.step.2.rest: from their phone. Those hours feed payroll in Financials — no notebook.
s6.step.3.rest: against the budget phases, from the same phone.
s6.step.4.bold: With no signal, everything keeps working:
s6.step.4.rest: clock-ins, photos and progress are saved on the phone and sync on their own when coverage returns.
